name: Robust Italian Stew: Wine-Braised Beef Over Creamy Polent
recipeIngredient: Beef Chuck Roast: 7 lbs, cut into 1-inch cubes
Beef Stock or Canned Broth: 2 1/4 cups
Whole Italian Plum Tomatoes: 32 oz, pitted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a heavy Dutch oven over high heat.
2. Add 1/5 of the beef and brown on all sides, stirring occasionally for about 5 minutes. Transfer the beef to a bowl using a slotted spoon. Repeat with the remaining beef in 4 more batches. Reduce heat to medium.
3. Add onion and chopped Italian parsley to the Dutch oven and cook until the onion is golden brown, stirring frequently for about 10 minutes.
4. Stir in the chopped garlic, bay leaves, ground cloves, cinnamon, and allspice. Return the beef to the Dutch oven and stir to coat with the onion and spice mixture.
5. Add red wine and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits. Simmer for 15 minutes. Add beef stock and simmer for another 10 minutes. Add the tomatoes (with their juices) and break them up with the back of a spoon. Mix in the olives and rosemary. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until the beef is tender, stirring occasionally, about 1 1/2 hours.
6. Add bell peppers, cover, and cook until just tender, about 15 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. (Can be prepared 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate.)
Polenta:
7. Bring water and salt to a boil in a heavy large saucepan. Gradually whisk in cornmeal. Continue whisking until the mixture boils and thickens, about 2 minutes. Reduce heat to low. Cook the cornmeal until it is thick and thoroughly cooked, whisking occasionally for about 30 minutes.
8. (Can be prepared up to 4 hours ahead. Transfer polenta to a metal bowl. Cover tightly with aluminum foil. Set the bowl over a large saucepan of barely-simmering water. Stir occasionally.)
